vision: Track every meaningful AI development tool on GitHub. 10K repos by end of March 2026. 100K by end of April 2026.

working.name: reporium.com
working.evidence: Live — {db_total} repos browseable, full-text search, mobile responsive

working.name: forksync v2
working.evidence: Cloud Run nightly sync + SYNC_REPORT.md committed via GitHub API after each run

working.name: reporium-db
working.evidence: Nightly sync active, {db_total} repos tracked, {db_languages} languages, GraphQL batch fetch

working.name: reporium-api
working.evidence: Deployed to Cloud Run — {api_repos_tracked} repos via REST API, Swagger UI public at /docs

working.name: reporium-events
working.evidence: Pub/Sub topic live, event schemas defined, forksync + reporium-db publishing events

working.name: reporium-audit
working.evidence: Nightly 8am UTC audit of all platform components, AUDIT_REPORT.md auto-generated

working.name: perditio-devkit
working.evidence: Shared tooling: badges, GitHub client, file utilities — used by all repos

not_working.name: reporium-ingestion
not_working.reason: Pipeline not running in cloud — 0 categories enriched, 0 readme summaries

not_working.name: AI categories
not_working.reason: Only 'tooling' exists in reporium-db — real AI categorization requires ingestion pipeline

fixing_now.name: reporium-ingestion pipeline
fixing_now.description: Fix cloud deployment — unblocks categories, summaries, reporium.com usefulness

fixing_now.name: 10K repos milestone
fixing_now.description: Expand GitHub fetcher to discover repos beyond perditioinc forks

coming_next.name: repo-intelligence
coming_next.description: 0-100 repo scorer: README, activity, community, CI. Pip-installable.

coming_next.name: github-ai-trends
coming_next.description: Daily GitHub trending scraper focused on AI repos. Scrapes github.com/trending per language (Python, TypeScript, Rust, Go, C++, Jupyter Notebook) for daily, weekly, and monthly time periods. Stores one timestamped snapshot per day. Feeds new repo candidates to reporium-db. Does not do AI enrichment — discovery only.

coming_next.name: reporium frontend v2
coming_next.description: Figma-designed UI: faster, cleaner, better filtering

backlog.name: reporium-trending
backlog.description: Multi-source trend aggregation across GitHub, LinkedIn, X, and other platforms. Requires dedicated scraping infrastructure and multiple data source integrations. Planned for a future phase after core platform is stable.
backlog.blocked_by: Requires ingestion pipeline, dedicated scraping infrastructure, and multiple third-party API integrations. Not started.

deleted_repos.name: reporium-trending
deleted_repos.reason: Incorrectly scoped. Was using GitHub Search API star counts and lookback days instead of scraping actual GitHub trending pages. Also misnamed — reporium-trending should eventually aggregate trends from multiple sources (GitHub, LinkedIn, X), not just GitHub. Replaced by github-ai-trends for GitHub-only trending.

changelog.notes: reporium-api deployed to Cloud Run ({api_repos_tracked} repos live), reporium-events Pub/Sub system, reporium-audit nightly health checks, perditio-devkit shared tooling, build counters on all nightly repos, Reporium suite badges across all repos — {test_count} tests passing
reporium-db, reporium-dataset, reporium-metrics, portfolio, repo-intelligence all launched — {test_count} tests passing across all repos
forksync v2 launched on Cloud Run (68s for 818 repos), reporium-ingestion pipeline built — not yet deployed to cloud
